task.name: Worker schema workflow
task.objective: Execute worker workflow using tmux-cli: create/reuse a tmux window, send a command or instructions to the agent, respond to questions, wait for completion, and optionally chain to a follow-up command.

parameters.param: The slash command to send to the agent (e.g. /bmad-bmm-quick-spec). Provided via consumer's <commandName>.
Argument to pass with the command (e.g. a file path or task ID). NULL means no argument. Provided via consumer's <argument>.
Tmux window name. If not provided, derived from {{COMMAND_NAME}}. Provided via consumer's <windowName>.
Multi-line instructions to send instead of a slash command. When provided, step 4 is used instead of step 3. Provided via consumer's <message>.
Behavioral constraints prepended to the message (e.g. "you CAN: ..., you CANNOT: ..."). Provided via consumer's <permissions>.
A command to execute after the agent completes successfully (e.g. /worker:tech-spec). Provided via consumer's <postCompletion>.

requirements.note: Consumer .md file MUST provide at least {{COMMAND_NAME}} inside a <context> block
Consumer may specify additional requirements (CLI tools, MCP servers) — respect those too

llm.mandate: Execute steps ONE BY ONE in EXACT ORDER — NEVER skip or reorder steps
Create TODO list for tracking all tasks
ALWAYS use windows-send for sending commands/instructions to the agent
ALWAYS send slash command and instruction as TWO SEPARATE windows-send calls — command first, then instruction — to ensure command loads before instruction is processed
ALWAYS use windows-message for responding to agent questions
NEVER use sleep to wait for responses — stay IDLE, the agent's message will trigger you

step.title: Create or Reuse Tmux Window
step.action: Call tmux-cli tool: windows-create with name from {{WINDOW_NAME}} (or derive from {{COMMAND_NAME}} if not provided)

check.action: Skip creation, mark window as REUSED

step.title: Clear Window if Reused
step.critical: ALWAYS use windows-send, NEVER use windows-message

substep.title: Send Slash Command
substep.action: Execute windows-send with ONLY the slash command: {{COMMAND_NAME}}
substep.critical: Send the slash command ALONE — no argument, no extra text. This ensures the command loads properly.
ALWAYS use windows-send, NEVER use windows-message

substep.title: Send Instruction / Argument
substep.action: Execute a SEPARATE windows-send with the argument/instruction
substep.note: CRITICAL: Provide EXHAUSTIVE DESCRIPTION as the argument — include all gathered context, file paths, and details
substep.critical: This MUST be a separate windows-send call from step 3.1 — never combine command and instruction in one call
ALWAYS use windows-send, NEVER use windows-message

step.title: Scope Guard — enforce single-purpose usage
step.rule: The agent should be used ONLY for what {{COMMAND_NAME}} is designed to do.
step.example: {{COMMAND_NAME}} says "create spec" → you use it to create a spec
{{COMMAND_NAME}} says "create spec" → you use it to implement code
step.critical: If the agent asks to do something beyond its intended purpose, answer NO

substep.title: Send Slash Command (if provided)
substep.action: Execute windows-send with ONLY the slash command: {{COMMAND_NAME}}
substep.critical: Send the slash command ALONE first — this ensures the command loads properly before instructions are sent.
ALWAYS use windows-send, NEVER use windows-message

substep.title: Send Instructions
substep.action: Compose the full message by combining {{PERMISSIONS}} (if provided) + {{MESSAGE}}
Execute a SEPARATE windows-send with the composed message
substep.note: This MUST be a separate windows-send from the slash command — never combine them
substep.critical: ALWAYS use windows-send, NEVER use windows-message

step.title: Send Instructions Only
step.condition: {{MESSAGE}} IS provided AND {{COMMAND_NAME}} IS NOT provided
step.action: Compose the full message by combining {{PERMISSIONS}} (if provided) + {{MESSAGE}}
Execute windows-send with the composed message
step.critical: ALWAYS use windows-send, NEVER use windows-message

step.title: Respond to Agent Questions

check.condition: agent asks about running/implementing beyond its scope
check.action: CRITICAL: Answer NO — the agent must stay within its designated task

check.condition: you have enough context to answer
check.action: RESPOND immediately using windows-message — DO NOT ask the user
check.reason: Minimize user interruptions when you can answer autonomously

check.action: Then execute windows-message with user's answer

step.critical: ALWAYS use windows-message for responses, NEVER use windows-send

step.title: Wait for Completion — stay IDLE
step.action: Do NOTHING until the agent signals that all work is done
step.note: Agent will tell you when work is complete
If agent asks to do more than it was intended for, say NO

step.title: Determine Result
step.action: Kill the window — agent completed successfully

action.condition: operation success, still more to do

step.action: Continue until all work is done
Ask the user how to proceed

step.title: Post-Completion
step.note: This allows automatic pipeline chaining — the next worker starts immediately after success

execution-rules.rule: windows-send: Use for sending commands and instructions to the agent
windows-message: Use for responding to agent questions

CRITICAL: Slash commands and instructions MUST be sent as TWO SEPARATE windows-send calls — first the command alone, then the instruction/argument in a second call. This ensures the command loads before instructions are processed.
Never skip wait steps — agent coordination is critical
Always check the consumer's <context> block for parameter values before starting

llm.critical-rules: • Use agents only for the task they are intended for • This is a WORKER TASK that coordinates with a tmux-cli agent • You MUST execute each step in exact order • You MUST wait for agent responses before proceeding (stay idle — the message will trigger you) • You MUST NOT use "sleep" to wait for messages — just stay idle • You MUST use the correct tool (windows-send vs windows-message) for each step • Create TODO list at start to track all steps
